mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand and spending, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, thereby supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward returns of the S&P 500 do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with the month-over-month growth of RSAFS over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer confidence and spending, which negatively impacts corporate profits and S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates an aggressive monetary tightening cycle aimed at curbing inflation, which typically leads to higher discount rates and reduced corporate valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change > 3% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.5%
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS show significant increases as per the trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of confirmed economic expansion, strong housing starts (HOUST) reflect robust investment and consumer confidence in the economy's future, driving demand for related industries and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month growth does not correlate positively with SPX 20-day returns during periods identified as economic expansion over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy manufacturing and supply chain activity, signaling strong underlying economic momentum that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with INDPRO month-over-month growth over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable unemployment rate (UNRATE) below a critical threshold indicates full employment conditions, which supports wage growth and robust consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of periods when UNRATE is consistently below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of increasing GDP (GDPC1) and robust retail sales (RSAFS) signifies broad-based economic strength and strong consumer confidence, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 1-quarter growth > 0.5% AND RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3%
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 30% of instances where both GDPC1 and RSAFS show strong growth as per the trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: During a high inflation environment, any increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is interpreted as a strong signal of future economic slowdown as the central bank attempts to cool the economy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS rises during a high inflation regime (CPIAUCSL 12-month change > 3%).
